text: The patient is positioned in lithotomy, and topical anesthesia is sprayed onto the perianal region. A total of five milliliters of anesthesia solution is injected subcutaneously in a circumanal manner, with 2.5 milliliters administered on each side of the perianal region.
The surgeon replaces the previous needle with a long spinal needle, preparing for further injection or manipulation in the perianal region.

The surgeon injects five ml of local anesthesia solution into each ischiorectal fossa, approximately six centimeters deep, ensuring the needle remains centered within the fossa without approaching the pudendal vessels.
The surgeon injects three ml of solution into the posterior presacral space, followed by two ml into the anterior sphincter complex, using a needle to administer the anesthesia.
After this we wait for one minute
A well-lubricated finger is inserted into the rectum, applying slight posterior traction to relax the sphincter complex, preparing the area for the commencement of surgery.
